title: [trustless://eil] Panel - Banks won’t go onchain until we fix privacy
description:
This panel moderated by Tom Teman (Ethereum Foundation) together with Fredrik (Ethereum Foundation), Arik Galansky (Fireblocks), Amzah Moelah (ABN AMRO), Jakub Lesniowski (UBS) discuss the fact that public blockchains are transparent by design - an uncomfortable reality for banks that handle confidential client and operational data. 

This panel unpacks why privacy is the missing piece preventing institutions from fully embracing onchain finance. Together with experts from leading banks and the Ethereum ecosystem, we’ll explore regulatory compliance through zero-knowledge proofs, user and institutional privacy, and the balance between transparency, trustlessness, and confidentiality in decentralized systems.

Talk given during day 1 of the trustless:// conference at Devconnect Argentina 2025

https://trustlessconference.com
